steps.title: Identify the passage context and the underlined portion's role
steps.content:
The passage opens by establishing that any young playwright arriving in Madrid must come to Don Jerónimo's home to get work produced.

The underlined portion then shifts focus to describe how he treats these playwrights: he receives them “with open arms,” yet “never spared the young authors” and “criticized vigorously, even to the verge of insult.” This description of his critical engagement appears after the statement of his necessity, showing what his support actually entails.

steps.title: Match the evidence to the purpose
steps.content:
The underlined portion describes Don Jerónimo's rigorous, critical approach—he doesn't simply approve work but actively critiques it harshly.

Since the passage has already established that playwrights depend on him for production, this detailed description of his demanding, vigorous criticism reveals that his support carries real weight; his approval (or feedback) shapes outcomes.

The other choices misread the function:
  • A wrongly claims he lacks interest despite clear evidence of his engagement
  • B shifts focus to his general views of theatrical quality rather than his impact on playwrights
  • C invents a talent contrast not present in the text
.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
